title: Add SHA256 checksum verification for livedocs binary download in action.yml
description: CRITICAL from Wave 1 convergence security review. action.yml is the primary adopter surface (every `uses: sjarmak/livedocs@...` runs it), but its binary download step has NO integrity check. Worktree 5vc.7 added checksum verification to examples/workflows/livedocs-prbot.yml only. Compromised release asset → code execution as root on adopter runners via sudo mv. Fix: apply the identical two-guard pattern (archive presence pre-check via grep + sha256sum --ignore-missing -c) to action.yml lines 88-133 for BOTH the tar.gz and zip branches (macOS gets zip; linux gets tar.gz).
notes:
PLAN: Mirror 5vc.7 two-guard checksum pattern in action.yml Download step. Add gh release download --pattern checksums.txt; grep -qF archive presence pre-check; sha256sum --ignore-missing -c before tar/unzip. set -euo pipefail so any failure aborts before sudo mv. Add TestActionYML_ChecksumVerification regression test. Both linux(tar.gz) and macOS(zip) covered by the same pre-extraction verification — one checksums.txt covers all archives.
PLAN-REVIEW: Self-conducted adversarial review. Key findings: (1) macOS runners have sha256sum via pre-installed coreutils in github-hosted macos-latest images - stock macOS lacks it but the action targets GH runners. (2) Guard 1 grep has theoretical substring-match weakening (e.g. matches .sig lines), but Guard 2 sha256sum -c reads the actual archive bytes so cryptographic verification holds regardless. Reference pattern remains sound. (3) Placing verification between gh download and tar/unzip ensures sudo mv never runs on unverified bytes under set -euo pipefail. (4) checksums.txt is release-wide so single verification covers both OS branches - no branch-specific crypto needed. Mirroring reference exactly; no deviations.
PROGRESS: Added TestActionYML_ChecksumVerification (RED first) asserting (a) checksums.txt downloaded, (b) grep -qF presence pre-check, (c) sha256sum --ignore-missing -c, (d) set -euo pipefail in Download step, (e) sha256sum precedes sudo mv install command. Implemented the two-guard pattern in action.yml Download step mirroring livedocs-prbot.yml: added set -euo pipefail, added --pattern checksums.txt to gh release download, inserted grep+sha256sum verification between download and extraction, added checksums.txt to the /tmp cleanup. All 10 action.yml tests GREEN, YAML parses, go vet clean.
REVIEW: Conducted all three review perspectives (code, security, YAML/GH Actions). Findings: (CODE) Tightened Guard-0 test to assert the literal `--pattern "checksums.txt"` on gh release download, catching refactors that could remove the download while leaving the string elsewhere. (SECURITY) No CRITICAL/HIGH introduced — change strictly tightens security over pre-existing zero-verification state. Analyzed grep substring-collision attack: Guard 1 has a theoretical false-positive on .sig-style suffix entries, but Guard 2 sha256sum -c still cryptographically verifies the actual downloaded archive bytes against its real manifest entry. The documented limitation (integrity not authenticity) matches the reference. set -euo pipefail ensures sudo mv cannot run on unverified bytes. (YAML) No syntax/quoting issues; subshell idiom clean; shell: bash portable across linux/macOS runners. All 10 action.yml regression tests PASS.
close_reason: DONE: Added SHA256 checksum verification to action.yml Download livedocs binary step, mirroring the two-guard pattern from 5vc.7 (examples/workflows/livedocs-prbot.yml). Changes: (1) gh release download now pulls checksums.txt alongside the archive via --pattern, (2) grep -qF presence pre-check closes sha256sum --ignore-missing silent-success bypass, (3) sha256sum --ignore-missing -c cryptographically verifies archive bytes before extraction, (4) set -euo pipefail ensures any guard failure aborts before sudo mv /tmp/livedocs /usr/local/bin/livedocs, (5) checksums.txt added to /tmp cleanup. Same verification covers both linux (tar.gz) and darwin (zip) branches — checksums.txt is release-wide. Added TestActionYML_ChecksumVerification regression test asserting: --pattern "checksums.txt", grep -qF pre-check, sha256sum -c, set -euo pipefail, and ordering (sha256sum before install command). All 10 action.yml validation tests pass. Key decision: mirrored reference exactly without adding macOS shasum fallback — GitHub-hosted macos-latest runners include coreutils.

title: Fix stale reference false positives: capitalized English words misidentified as code symbols
description: The drift detector treats capitalized English words in markdown prose (e.g. Backward, Breaking, Communication) as Go symbols and flags them as stale when not found in code. This causes 60-70% false positive rate on stale references. Root cause is in the markdown-to-symbol extraction logic. Fix: only match words that appear in backticks, follow code-reference patterns (pkg.Symbol, camelCase, snake_case), or are confirmed against the actual symbol index. Discovered during x40.11 validation across 16 OSS repos.
notes:
PLAN: Root cause is goIdentRe in drift.go line 63 matching ANY PascalCase word from prose, filtered only by a static commonWords map (which can never be complete). Fix: change prose extraction to only match words with code-like patterns (contains dot, mixed case after first char like camelCase, contains underscore, or has 2+ internal uppercase). Backtick-quoted identifiers already handled correctly. Will add isCodeLikeIdentifier() function and update the prose extraction block.
PROGRESS: Replaced goIdentRe + commonWords blocklist approach with proseCodeRefRe that uses structural pattern matching. Prose words are now only treated as symbols if they contain dots (pkg.Symbol), camelCase, snake_case, or PascalCase with multiple uppercase transitions (ConfigMap, NewInformerFunc). Backtick extraction unchanged. All 18 tests pass, zero regressions. K8s validation shows significant false positive reduction.
close_reason: DONE: Replaced blocklist-based prose symbol extraction (goIdentRe + 130-entry commonWords map) with structural pattern matching (proseCodeRefRe). Prose words are now only treated as code symbols if they match code-like patterns: dotted refs (pkg.Symbol), camelCase, snake_case, or PascalCase with multiple uppercase transitions (ConfigMap). Plain capitalized English words like Backward, Breaking, Communication are no longer false positives. Backtick extraction unchanged. 4 new test cases added, all 18 tests pass with zero regressions.

Measure: time to first finding, signal-to-noise ratio, false positive rate. Decision gate: if <3/10 engage on original repos, revisit product strategy.
notes:
CONTEXT: This is market validation, not code. Clone 10 repos, run livedocs check, capture findings. For each repo: (1) record total findings, (2) manually classify each as true positive or false positive, (3) file a GitHub issue on the repo with the drift report, (4) track if maintainer responds/engages. Decision gate: <3/10 maintainer engagement = revisit product strategy.
SCOPE EXPANSION: Added adsabs GitHub org (Python-heavy astrophysics repos — tests cross-language support beyond Go) and sourcegraph/sourcegraph monorepo (large Go+TS codebase with heavy documentation — stress test for scale). These complement the original 10 Go-focused repos by validating livedocs works across languages and repo sizes.
PLAN: Build livedocs, shallow-clone repos into /tmp/livedocs-validation/, run 'livedocs check' on each, capture timing and structured JSON output. Three groups: (1) 10 Go repos, (2) 3-5 adsabs repos with most markdown, (3) sourcegraph monorepo.
PROGRESS: Completed validation against all 16 repos (10 Go, 5 adsabs, 1 sourcegraph monorepo). All repos cloned --depth=1. livedocs check run on every repo in both text and JSON format. Results collected: timing, stale refs, undocumented exports, false positive analysis.
RESULTS SUMMARY:

== KEY METRICS ==
- Time to first finding: <10ms on all repos (sub-second for every repo)
- Largest repo (grafana, 994 md files): 598ms
- Sourcegraph monorepo (917 md files): 642ms

== FALSE POSITIVE ANALYSIS ==
Stale refs false positive rate (heuristic: English words treated as symbols):
- cobra: 70% FP rate (548/778 are English words like 'Backward', 'Breaking', 'Cobra')
- prometheus: 61% FP rate (2779/4532 are English words)

Undocumented exports quality: MUCH better — these are real Go exported symbols (e.g. Configs.MarshalYAML, NewManager, StaticConfig) not mentioned in READMEs. True positive rate near 100%.

adsabs repos: 0 undocumented exports found because livedocs only supports Go code extraction, not Python.

== SIGNAL-TO-NOISE RATIO ==
- Stale references: LOW signal (60-70% false positives). Root cause: symbol extractor treats capitalized English words in markdown as code references.
- Undocumented exports: HIGH signal (near 100% true positive). Genuinely exported Go symbols missing from docs.
- Stale package refs: MODERATE signal. Some false positives from path-like strings (e.g. 'and/or', 'key/value').
- Cross-language: No Python/TS symbol extraction yet — adsabs repos only produce stale ref noise.

== CRITICAL FINDING ==
The stale reference detector has a fundamental false positive problem: it extracts capitalized words from markdown prose and flags them as 'code symbols not found in code.' This makes the stale ref count essentially meaningless for repos with narrative documentation (CHANGELOGs, CONTRIBUTINGs, etc.). The undocumented export detector is solid.
close_reason: DONE: Validated livedocs check against 16 repos (10 Go, 5 adsabs/Python, 1 sourcegraph monorepo). Performance excellent: sub-second for all repos including 994-md-file grafana (598ms) and sourcegraph monorepo (642ms). Undocumented export detection is high-quality (near 100% TP rate for Go repos). CRITICAL ISSUE: stale reference detection has 60-70% false positive rate — extracts capitalized English words from markdown prose as 'symbols.' Cross-language support absent (Python/TS repos produce 0 undocumented findings). Decision gate: product is NOT ready for external outreach until stale ref FP rate is fixed.

description: Run go/packages with NeedTypes|NeedDeps on 500 packages from kubernetes/kubernetes. Measure RSS peak. If >8GB, implement topological-layer extraction with intermediate disk writes. Must happen in week 1, not month 3. Premortem theme 3: 3/5 lenses predicted 14GB+ RSS explosion.

title: tribal_mine.go: propagate FailedCount/FailedErrors from MiningResult to MCP response
description: HIGH from Wave 1 convergence code review. After m7v.14 adds FailedCount + FailedErrors []error to MiningResult, mcpserver/tribal_mine.go handler iterates r.Facts but never inspects the new fields. tool can return 'No new tribal facts mined' when partial upsert failures silently dropped work. Contract violation: m7v.14 requires callers to check FailedCount>0. Fix: propagate FailedCount to JSON response envelope + log FailedErrors server-side. Blocks on m7v.14 merge.
notes:
PLAN: Add failed_count field to tribalMineResponse. Aggregate MiningResult.FailedCount across results. Log FailedErrors server-side via log.Printf with %q quoting. When Total==0 but FailedCount>0, return warning text that reports partial failure (not "No new tribal facts mined"). Do NOT expose raw FailedErrors in JSON (m7v.21 tracks sanitization). Tests: factUpserter injection via custom factory OR pre-seeded fact to force duplicate upsert. Files: mcpserver/tribal_mine.go, mcpserver/tribal_mine_test.go. Out of scope: FailedErrors sanitization (m7v.21), rate limiting (m7v.22).
PLAN: Add failed_count field to tribalMineResponse. Aggregate MiningResult.FailedCount across results. Log FailedErrors server-side via log.Printf with %q quoting. When Total==0 but FailedCount>0, return warning text that reports partial failure (not "No new tribal facts mined"). Do NOT expose raw FailedErrors in JSON (m7v.21 tracks sanitization). Tests: factUpserter injection via custom factory OR pre-seeded fact to force duplicate upsert. Files: mcpserver/tribal_mine.go, mcpserver/tribal_mine_test.go. Out of scope: FailedErrors sanitization (m7v.21), rate limiting (m7v.22).
PLAN-REVIEW: Self-administered architect review (Task agent unavailable). (1) additive failed_count JSON field is backward-compat — existing clients ignore unknown fields. (2) Raw FailedErrors must NOT be exposed to client per m7v.21 security concern — log only, use %q quoting to defeat log injection. (3) "No new tribal facts mined" path must report failure count when FailedCount>0 — core contract violation. (4) Tool returns SUCCESS with failed_count>0 when some facts mined; returns text warning (not error) when zero facts + failures — keeps plumbing simple and lets agent decide via the numeric field. (5) Log format: "tribal_mine_on_demand: repo=%q path=%q failed_count=%d first_error=%q" with %q quoting.
close_reason: DONE: tribalMineResponse now includes failed_count (json-tagged) aggregated from MiningResult.FailedCount across all results. Extracted buildTribalMineResponse helper for testability. logMiningFailures emits one server-side log line per failing result with %q-quoted repo/path/first_error (defeats log injection). Partial-failure path (Total==0, FailedCount>0) returns a warning text that reports the count instead of the misleading "No new tribal facts mined" message — this is the core m7v.14 contract fix. Raw FailedErrors never serialized to client; sanitization tracked separately by m7v.21. 4 new tests (facts+failures, zero-facts+failures, zero-facts+zero-failures unchanged, nil results). gofmt+vet clean; mcpserver + extractor/tribal tests pass with and without -race.

title: Add SHA256 checksum verification for livedocs binary download in workflow template
description: examples/workflows/livedocs-prbot.yml downloads the livedocs binary from GitHub releases with no integrity check. If sjarmak/livedocs is compromised, every adopter's CI would execute attacker-controlled code with GITHUB_TOKEN and SRC_ACCESS_TOKEN access. Fix: publish checksums.txt alongside each release (goreleaser can do this), then add sha256sum -c verification step. Consider also changing template default from LIVEDOCS_VERSION: latest to a pinned version. From convergence security review.
notes:
PLAN: Add checksums.txt download + sha256sum -c verification in examples/workflows/livedocs-prbot.yml before tar extract. .goreleaser.yaml already publishes checksums.txt (sha256) — no change needed. Keeping LIVEDOCS_VERSION=latest default; checksum is integrity-only (not authenticity), GPG signing is a potential follow-up bead. Fail-closed: download step and sha256sum -c both hard-fail under set -euo pipefail.
PROGRESS: added checksum verification to examples/workflows/livedocs-prbot.yml. Two guards: (1) grep -F asserts our archive is listed in checksums.txt (closes the --ignore-missing silent-pass bypass); (2) sha256sum --ignore-missing -c validates the hash. Both run before tar extraction under set -euo pipefail so any failure aborts the job and the cache write. .goreleaser.yaml already emits checksums.txt (sha256) — no change there. Kept LIVEDOCS_VERSION=latest default; documented that this is integrity-only, not authenticity (GPG/cosign is follow-up).
REVIEW: 3 review passes complete. code-reviewer: 0 findings — ordering correct (verify before extract), set -euo pipefail propagates failures, grep -qF closes sha256sum --ignore-missing silent-pass bypass. security-reviewer: 1 MEDIUM — integrity only, not authenticity (compromised release repo can publish matching pair). Filed follow-up live_docs-5vc.11 for cosign/GPG signing. Empirically tested sha256sum --ignore-missing edge cases (tampered file fails; absent-from-checksums is the bypass we closed with explicit grep). goreleaser review: no changes to .goreleaser.yaml — existing checksum block already emits sha256 checksums.txt and existing goreleaser_test.go TestGoreleaserConfig/checksum_sha256 still passes.
close_reason: DONE: added sha256 checksum verification to examples/workflows/livedocs-prbot.yml — downloads checksums.txt alongside tarball, asserts our archive is listed (closes --ignore-missing silent-pass bypass), runs sha256sum -c before tar extract, all under set -euo pipefail so failure aborts the step and the cache write. .goreleaser.yaml already emits checksums.txt (sha256) so no goreleaser change needed. Kept LIVEDOCS_VERSION=latest default with explicit comment that this is integrity-only. Filed follow-up live_docs-5vc.11 for cosign/GPG signing to close the authenticity gap (compromised release repo can still publish a matching pair).

title: livedocs check scans all markdown files, causing false-positive flood
description:
livedocs check walks every .md file in the repo and treats symbol references as documentation for the current Go package. On the livedocs repo itself this produces 28,909 stale references and 4,796 stale package refs because PRDs, design docs, brainstorm files, and worktree copies reference symbols from other codebases (Kubernetes, client-go, etc.).

Current mitigation: fail-threshold set to -1 (informational only) in example-livedocs.yml.

Root cause: drift.Detect() in drift/drift.go compares README symbols against ExtractGoExports(codeDir) where codeDir defaults to the README directory. No filtering of non-documentation markdown (PRDs, design docs, .brainstorm/, .claude/worktrees/).

Acceptance criteria:
- livedocs check respects a .livedocsignore file OR scopes to README.md/AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md only
- Alternative: add --include/--exclude glob flags to livedocs check
- On the livedocs repo, livedocs check with default settings produces < 50 stale refs (not 28,909)
- CI drift check can run with fail-threshold 0 without false-positive failures

Options to consider:
1. .livedocsignore file (like .gitignore syntax) excluding .brainstorm/, docs/design/, .claude/
2. Only scan files matching a pattern (README*.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md)
3. --exclude flag on the CLI
4. Combination: sensible defaults + override via ignore file
notes:
PLAN: Combination approach - (1) default doc file patterns (README*.md, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CHANGELOG.md) instead of all *.md, (2) .livedocsignore file support with gitignore-style patterns, (3) --exclude/--include/--all-markdown CLI flags. Files: check/check.go, check/check_test.go, cmd/livedocs/check.go, example-livedocs.yml.
PROGRESS: Implemented FindDocFiles with default doc patterns (README.md, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, etc.), .livedocsignore support, --exclude/--include/--all-markdown CLI flags. All 30 tests pass. CI workflow updated to fail-threshold 0.
REVIEW: Self-reviewed all changes. No CRITICAL/HIGH issues. Fixed one MEDIUM: safe slice allocation in FindDocFiles to prevent potential mutation. All 30 tests pass including audit package.
close_reason: DONE: Scoped drift detection to known doc file patterns (README, CLAUDE, AGENTS, etc.) with .livedocsignore override and --exclude/--include/--all-markdown CLI flags. Rejects traversal and invalid glob patterns. CI fail-threshold restored to 0.

title: Tribal facts: symbol-level linking for MCP tools
description: Tribal extractors currently create file-level subjects (file paths as symbol names) rather than linking to existing per-symbol entries in the symbols table. This means MCP tools like tribal_owners('ClaimsDB') return nothing because facts are keyed to 'db/claims.go' not 'ClaimsDB'. Fix: (1) MCP tribal tools should accept file paths and resolve them to all symbols in that file, OR (2) extractors should link facts to individual symbols when possible (blame has line ranges, inline markers have line numbers that can be matched to symbol ranges). Approach 2 is better long-term but approach 1 is needed as a fallback regardless.
notes:
PLAN: Two-pass lookup in queryTribalFactsForSymbol. Pass 1: search by symbol_name (current). Pass 2 fallback: if symbols found but no tribal facts, look up source_file from claims table, then search for file-path symbols with tribal facts. Changes: mcpserver/tribal_tools.go, mcpserver/tribal_tools_test.go, db/tribal.go (new GetTribalFactsBySourceFile). No extractor changes needed.
PROGRESS: Implemented two-pass lookup. Pass 1: direct symbol name match. Pass 2: resolve import_path to local dir prefix, find file-level tribal subjects. E2E verified: tribal_owners('ClaimsDB') now returns 4 ownership facts from db/ package files.
REVIEW: Fixed 2 HIGH issues from go-reviewer: (1) escaped LIKE wildcards in GetTribalFactSubjectsByPathPrefix to prevent wildcard injection, (2) used per-repo priorCount instead of global resp.Facts for fallback predicate. Removed unused repoName param from importPathToLocalDir.
close_reason: DONE: Two-pass lookup in MCP tribal tools. Pass 1: direct symbol name. Pass 2: resolve import_path to local dir prefix, find file-level tribal subjects. tribal_owners('ClaimsDB') now returns 4 ownership facts. LIKE injection prevented via escapeLike helper. All tests pass.

title: Evaluate wazero WASM runtime for tree-sitter (replace CGO)
description: Prototype tree-sitter-go via wazero WASM runtime. Measure: parse speed (must be <500ms/file), memory overhead, grammar version pinning. Compare against CGO bindings. Eliminates CGO build fragility and cross-platform issues. Premortem theme 2: CGO dependency identified as fragile by 2/5 lenses.
notes:
PLAN: Evaluate 3 tree-sitter Go approaches: (1) smacker/go-tree-sitter (CGO baseline), (2) malivvan/tree-sitter (wazero-based, exactly matching PRD spec), (3) odvcencio/gotreesitter (pure Go, no CGO, 436 stars, 206 grammars). Benchmark on 5 k8s Go files (52-8529 lines). Measure: parse time, memory alloc, correctness (AST node count + function extraction). Verdict criteria: wazero must parse <500ms/file per PRD.
RESULTS: CGO (smacker/go-tree-sitter) is 12-7500x faster than pure Go (odvcencio/gotreesitter). CGO: 85us-8ms range. PureGo: 1.7ms-40s range. types.go (8529 lines) takes 40s in pure Go vs 5ms in CGO. kubelet.go (3579 lines) borderline at 500ms. Correctness: function extraction matches 100%. malivvan/tree-sitter (wazero) only supports C/C++ grammars at v0.0.1 -- unusable for Go.
close_reason: DONE: Wazero WASM is NOT viable — the only Go library (malivvan/tree-sitter v0.0.1) lacks Go grammar support. Pure Go alternative (odvcencio/gotreesitter v0.12.2) is 12-7500x slower than CGO with 310MB+ memory on large files, failing the <500ms PRD requirement on files >3000 lines. CGO tree-sitter (smacker/go-tree-sitter) remains the only viable option at 85us-8ms parse times. Verdict: keep CGO with static linking + Docker build mitigation. Re-evaluate when wazero ecosystem matures.

title: ADR: Claims-backed generation with verification gates
description:
Decision: Store documentation as structured claims (tuples) in SQLite, not as prose files. Three-tier model: Tier 1 (AST-derived, auto-ship), Tier 2 (LLM semantic, verification gate), Tier 3 (human-written, drift-detect only).

Rationale: Emerged from structured debate between Detection-First, Generation-First, and Structured Claims DB positions. All three converged on claims as the integration layer. Detection provides trust infrastructure. Generation fills the 94% absence gap. Claims DB makes both composable.

Research: 5-agent divergent research + 3-advocate convergent debate. See convergence_report.md and prd_live_documentation_system.md.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (post-premortem revision): Claims-backed generation with verification gates adopted. Key premortem modifications: (1) stateless hook architecture, (2) usefulness validation gate before building, (3) Tier 1 redefined as 'enhanced go doc' not GoDoc duplicate, (4) Phase 0 added for output validation. See premortem_live_docs_architecture.md themes 3-5.

title: ADR: Use SCIP symbol format, not SCIP tooling
description:
Decision: Adopt SCIP's symbol string format as canonical ID but build primary extractor on Go stdlib (go/packages + go/types + go/doc). Use tree-sitter for incremental hot path. Optionally consume SCIP indexes for cross-repo resolution.

Rationale: scip-go wraps the same Go stdlib packages, adds 60% irrelevant data (syntax highlighting, diagnostics), breaks on every Go release, has no incremental indexing, and is pre-1.0. go/doc is purpose-built for documentation extraction. SCIP symbol format is valuable for cross-repo joins across 79 kubernetes repos.

Research: 5-agent divergent research (feasibility, data model, incremental, multi-repo, alternatives). See prd_scip_implementation.md.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (post-premortem revision): SCIP symbol format demoted from primary key to secondary index. Primary identity is composite repo+import_path+name. Rationale: 3/5 premortem lenses identified SCIP format as highest-confidence risk (staging replace variants, pre-1.0 spec drift, Go-specific encoding). WASM tree-sitter replaces CGO bindings. title: Build content-hash caching layer
description: SHA-256 hash per source file stored in source_files table. On each commit, compute hashes for changed files only. Skip extraction for unchanged files. Turns O(n) into O(changed). Essential for making per-commit updates affordable.
notes:
PREMORTEM UPDATE: Cache key must include content_hash + extractor_version + grammar_version. Add LRU eviction at 2GB cap. Add deletion-aware reconciliation (tombstone on git rm). See premortem themes 2-3.
PLAN: Content-hash caching layer design.

## Cache Key
Composite: SHA256(file_content) + extractor_version + grammar_version
- Stored in source_files table per PRD schema
- Cache hit = all three match → skip extraction

## LRU Eviction (2GB cap)
- Track total cache size via per-entry byte count stored in source_files
- On insert, if total > 2GB, evict least-recently-used entries (by last_indexed timestamp)
- Eviction removes source_files row AND associated claims

## Deletion-Aware Reconciliation
- On each git diff, identify deleted/renamed files
- Mark deleted files with deleted=1 tombstone (soft delete)
- Tombstoned entries excluded from queries, eligible for eviction first
- Reconcile() scans git diff output, marks removals, inserts new files

## Core API
- CacheStore interface: Get/Put/Delete/Reconcile/Evict
- SQLite-backed implementation
- Get(repo, path, contentHash, extractorVer, grammarVer) → (cached bool)
- Put(repo, path, contentHash, extractorVer, grammarVer, sizeBytes)
- MarkDeleted(repo, path)
- Reconcile(repo, currentFiles map[path]hash, deletedPaths []string)
- Evict() → evict LRU until under 2GB cap

## Implementation Files
- /home/ds/live_docs/cache/cache.go — interface + types
- /home/ds/live_docs/cache/sqlite.go — SQLite implementation
- /home/ds/live_docs/cache/cache_test.go — tests
PROGRESS: Implementation complete. 21 tests pass, 76.4% coverage, race-free, go vet clean. Files: cache/cache.go (interface+types), cache/sqlite.go (SQLite impl), cache/cache_test.go (21 tests). Pure-Go SQLite via modernc.org/sqlite (no CGO).
close_reason: DONE: Content-hash caching layer implemented in Go under /home/ds/live_docs/cache/. Three files: cache.go (Store interface + Entry type), sqlite.go (SQLite-backed implementation with WAL mode, pure-Go via modernc.org/sqlite), cache_test.go (21 tests). Cache key is composite SHA-256(content) + extractor_version + grammar_version per PRD spec. LRU eviction at configurable cap (default 2GB) with tombstoned entries evicted first. Deletion-aware reconciliation: Reconcile() diffs current files against cache, tombstones missing files, returns changed/new paths for re-extraction. All tests pass with -race flag. 76.4% statement coverage (remaining gaps are defensive SQL error branches). go vet clean.

title: mcpserver: add Cause() accessor to ToolResult interface (vs concrete-type assertion)
description: Review follow-up from live_docs-m7v.26 (wave-3 go-reviewer M1 + partial H1). Current ResultCause free-function type-asserts to *resultAdapter directly, so foreign ToolResult implementations (e.g., test doubles in other packages) cannot carry a cause and silently return nil. Also: resultAdapter.Unwrap returns *mcp.CallToolResult rather than satisfying errors.Unwrap() error convention, which creates silent false-negatives for callers who use errors.Is directly on a ToolResult (mitigated by godoc warning in m7v.26 but design debt remains). Fix: either (a) extend ToolResult with a Cause() error method so the accessor works uniformly across implementations, or (b) introduce a narrow package-private 'causer' interface and type-assert to that in ResultCause. Renaming Unwrap -> RawResult() (or similar) to free the name for the standard errors convention is a larger breaking change; scope this bead to the Cause() accessor pattern and file the rename separately if needed.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=56eb84ce4cfaa59983f5c735b9ac6ef510608550 contains main=56eb84ce4cfaa59983f5c735b9ac6ef510608550 (fast-forwarded onto main)

PLAN: Adopt option (b'): introduce exported package-level interface 'Causer' with single method 'Cause() error' in mcpserver/adapter.go. Modify ResultCause to first try the new Causer interface (works for any implementation including foreign test doubles), then fall back to *resultAdapter for compat (or simply rely on Causer since *resultAdapter will satisfy it). Make *resultAdapter satisfy Causer by adding Cause() method. ToolResult remains unchanged so existing implementers don't break. Files: mcpserver/adapter.go (interface + method + ResultCause refactor), mcpserver/adapter_cause_test.go (new TDD file). Tests: nil ToolResult, NewTextResult/NewErrorResult => nil cause, NewErrorResultWithCause => cause, foreign type implementing ToolResult+Causer => cause, foreign ToolResult without Causer => nil. PLAN-REVIEW: no changes. Backward-compat preserved (additive Causer interface, ToolResult unchanged). Nil-safety: ResultCause(nil) and Causer.Cause()==nil both yield nil. Existing errors.Is callers unaffected. The Causer path subsumes the *resultAdapter fallback once *resultAdapter implements Cause(); type-assertion fallback can be dropped for cleanliness.

title: cmd/livedocs: replace package-level flag vars with cmd.Flags().GetX() inside RunE (root-cause refactor)
description: Root-cause refactor surfaced by go-reviewer during Wave 1 convergence review on m7v.28. The defer-reset pattern in verify_claims.go (m7v.28) and the planned same fix in m7v.33 (enrich) are symptom-level — the real issue is package-level mutable flag vars (verifyClaimsDB, verifyClaimsStaleness, verifyClaimsCanary, verifyClaimsCheckEx in verify_claims.go and similar in other commands). Idiomatic cobra reads flags inside RunE via cmd.Flags().GetBool("check-existing") etc. — which eliminates global state entirely and makes flag-leak bugs impossible by construction. Tradeoff rejected by m7v.28 PLAN-REVIEW: factory approach requiring rootCmd rebuild. This bead proposes a narrower alternative: keep rootCmd, but replace each command's package-level vars with local flag reads. Lower blast radius than factory, eliminates the need for reset helpers in m7v.28/m7v.33, addresses go-reviewer's concurrency-safety concern (package vars aren't goroutine-safe if ever called concurrently). Scope: cmd/livedocs/*.go — enumerate and migrate per-command. Likely large diff across ~8-10 files. Should land AFTER m7v.33 (so pattern has 3 instances proving duplication, justifying extraction).

title: Surface tribal.ErrMineThrottled distinctly in TribalMineOnDemandHandler
description: Wave 3 convergence code review (MEDIUM CR2) on live_docs-m7v.23. When live_docs-m7v.17 lands, MineSymbol/MineFile can return *tribal.MiningError{Code:mine_throttled, Err:ErrMineThrottled}. TribalMineOnDemandHandler currently renders this through MiningError.SafeMessage() which returns 'per-file mining rate limit reached; retry shortly' — acceptable, but callers still get it as a generic tool-error result rather than a distinguishable retry-after signal. Fix: detect errors.Is(err, tribal.ErrMineThrottled) in the handler and emit a response with a structured Retry-After hint (via MCP annotations or a distinct result code), separate from 'mining service unavailable' shaped errors. This lets MCP clients implement backoff correctly. Tests: TestTribalMineOnDemand_ThrottleYieldsDistinctResponse.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=56eb84ce4cfaa59983f5c735b9ac6ef510608550 contains main=56eb84ce4cfaa59983f5c735b9ac6ef510608550
PLAN: Extract a small renderMineError(err) ToolResult helper from TribalMineOnDemandHandler to centralize the classification of mining errors. Add an errors.Is(err, tribal.ErrMineThrottled) branch BEFORE the generic *MiningError catch so throttles are surfaced via NewErrorResultWithCause(text, tribal.ErrMineThrottled) — clients distinguish via errors.Is(ResultCause(r), tribal.ErrMineThrottled). User-visible text reuses MiningError.SafeMessage() phrasing ('per-file mining rate limit reached; retry shortly'). Pattern mirrors the rate-limited handlers ErrRateLimited cause attachment. Files: mcpserver/tribal_mine.go (extract helper + new branch), mcpserver/tribal_mine_test.go (TestTribalMineOnDemand_ThrottleYieldsDistinctResponse + classifier coverage). Out of scope: MineSymbol propagation (currently swallows non-budget errors — handler defends in case future versions propagate); ErrRateLimited sentinel relocation (m7v.39); MineLogger injection into plain handler (m7v.31).
PLAN-REVIEW: (1) errors.Is correctness verified — MiningError.Unwrap returns e.Err, and the throttled MiningError carries Err=ErrMineThrottled (extractor/tribal/service.go:349-357), so errors.Is(err, tribal.ErrMineThrottled) traverses correctly. (2) Backward compat: same human text, only typed cause added — cause is server-side-only per adapter.go:228-230 docs, never crosses wire. (3) Branch order: ErrMineThrottled check first, then context.Cancel/DeadlineExceeded, then generic *MiningError, then default — no overlap (throttled has only ErrMineThrottled in chain). (4) No race: helper is pure function on err. (5) No mcp-go imports added — using existing NewErrorResultWithCause. (6) Scope: handler only; tribal package untouched. No structural plan changes.
PROGRESS: Extracted renderMineError(err) ToolResult helper from TribalMineOnDemandHandler. Added errors.Is(err, tribal.ErrMineThrottled) branch (FIRST in classification order) that returns NewErrorResultWithCause with tribal.ErrMineThrottled as typed cause. User-visible text: 'tribal_mine_on_demand: per-file mining rate limit reached; retry after a short pause' — extends MiningError.SafeMessage(mine_throttled) with retry hint, mirroring rate-limited handler phrasing. Other branches (context.Canceled/DeadlineExceeded, generic *MiningError, fallback) preserved with no behavior change. New tests: TestTribalMineOnDemand_ThrottleYieldsDistinctResponse (asserts errors.Is(ResultCause(r), tribal.ErrMineThrottled), text safety, no path leak, no sentinel.Error() leak); TestTribalMineOnDemand_NonThrottleErrorHasNoThrottleCause (regression guard — budget_exceeded/extraction_failed/context.Canceled/unstructured all have nil throttle cause). go build clean, go test ./mcpserver/... ./extractor/tribal/... GREEN, go test -race ./mcpserver/... GREEN, go vet clean.

title: Export ErrRateLimited sentinel for tribal_mine_on_demand denial (replace string-match in callers/tests)
description: Wave 2 convergence go-review (MEDIUM G1) on live_docs-m7v.22. Rate-limit denial in TribalMineOnDemandRateLimitedHandler returns NewErrorResult(...) whose text must be string-matched (e.g., strings.Contains(text, 'rate')) to distinguish from budget-exceeded or transport errors. Fragile against wording changes. Fix: export mcpserver.ErrRateLimited sentinel; attach it as a typed cause on the denial result (via NewErrorResultWithCause or similar adapter helper) so middleware and tests can use errors.Is(err, mcpserver.ErrRateLimited). Update existing string-match tests to use errors.Is. Document the sentinel in tribal_mine.go and in the MCP server README.
notes:
BASE-VERIFIED: HEAD=02b140570f933af59c3d291e3dee2cab1b9abf41 contains main=02b140570f933af59c3d291e3dee2cab1b9abf41
PLAN: (1) declare var ErrRateLimited in mcpserver/tribal_mine.go; (2) extend resultAdapter with cause field + add NewErrorResultWithCause + ResultCause helper in adapter.go (keep ToolResult interface stable — use type-assertion inside ResultCause); (3) update rate-limit denial in TribalMineOnDemandRateLimitedHandler to use NewErrorResultWithCause(ErrRateLimited, <existing text>); (4) migrate tests in tribal_mine_ratelimit_test.go primary discriminator to errors.Is(mcpserver.ResultCause(r), ErrRateLimited) while keeping text smoke checks as secondary; (5) doc the sentinel in tribal_mine.go godoc (no mcpserver README exists; do not create one). Scope: mcpserver/tribal_mine.go, mcpserver/adapter.go, mcpserver/tribal_mine_ratelimit_test.go. Out of scope: TribalMineOnDemandHandler (m7v.30/31).
PLAN-REVIEW: (a) Keeping ToolResult interface stable avoids breaking other handlers. (b) Cause remains server-side — adaptHandler only sends result.Unwrap() to mcp-go transport, so cause doesn't leak. (c) errors.Is works because *resultAdapter carries the cause and ResultCause retrieves via type assertion. (d) Scope check: only RateLimited wrapper's denial path + adapter plumbing + tests. TribalMineOnDemandHandler untouched → no m7v.30/31 conflict. (e) Sentinel placement: mcpserver package (same as ErrLLMClientUnavailable) — correct, keeps MCP-layer concerns in MCP package. (f) No README to update; godoc comment on the sentinel + resultAdapter fields is the canonical doc.

Noted pre-existing TestGoreleaserConfig failure on main (archive_formats + homebrew_tap_configured) that is unrelated to this bead's scope; likely already tracked under 5vc.x goreleaser work.

title: KeyedLimiter: persist bucket state across LRU eviction to defeat thrash reset
description: Wave 2 convergence security review (MEDIUM M2) on live_docs-m7v.22. KeyedLimiter evicts LRU entries when MaxKeys is reached; the evicted bucket's rate.Limiter is discarded and a new one is created fresh on next use. Attacker with MaxKeys+1 distinct IDs can continuously evict legitimate sessions, and each re-insertion grants a fresh burst — effectively resetting the victim's partial-drain state. Fix: before evicting an lruEntry, snapshot (remaining_tokens, last_seen_time) and restore when the same key returns. Alternative: grow limiter map to max-reasonable-concurrent-sessions AND document MaxKeys as security-critical (partial doc added in m7v.22 commit). Files: extractor/tribal/limiter.go. Add regression test: TestKeyedLimiter_ThrashDoesNotRefreshBurstState.
notes:
PLAN: Add bounded snapshot LRU cache preserving *rate.Limiter pointer across eviction. On eviction, move limiter to snapshot cache (LRU-bounded by MaxSnapshotKeys, defaults to MaxKeys; TTL-bounded to bound memory). On (re-)insertion, restore from snapshot if present, else create fresh. This preserves (tokens, last_refill_time) across eviction — rate.Limiter continues refilling based on wall-clock, so no manual field surgery needed. Files: extractor/tribal/limiter.go + limiter_test.go. Required test: TestKeyedLimiter_ThrashDoesNotRefreshBurstState. Must update existing TestKeyedLimiter_LRUEvictionBoundsMap whose 'fresh bucket after eviction' assertion encodes the bug we are fixing.
PLAN-REVIEW: Adversarial self-review (architect): (A1) Snapshot cache is O(MaxKeys) bounded → no new DoS surface; (A2) Skip TTL — LRU bound is sufficient, KISS; (A3) Snapshot ops under same mutex = safe; (A4) Clock skew: rate.Limiter uses time.Now — restoring old pointer naturally refills correctly; (A5) TestKeyedLimiter_LRUEvictionBoundsMap assertion 'sess-1 fresh bucket after eviction' encodes the bug — must invert to 'sess-1 should still be denied (state preserved)'; (A6) Required test must use tiny Rate to prevent refill masking; (A7) Alternative (grow MaxKeys) is weaker — going with snapshot/restore; (A8) Preserving *rate.Limiter pointer preserves Rate/Burst/Tokens; (A9) Config static at construction — no drift; (A10) Reuse MaxKeys for snapshot bound — simpler API, document 2x memory.
PROGRESS: Fix implemented in limiter.go. Added snapshot LRU cache (same-size MaxKeys) that preserves *rate.Limiter on eviction and restores on re-insertion. Key subtle fix: popSnapshotLocked must run BEFORE the active-LRU eviction loop, otherwise evicting 2nd+ attackers into the snapshot cache can push the victim's own snapshot out. Added 3 new tests (ThrashDoesNotRefreshBurstState, SnapshotCacheIsBounded, SnapshotRestorePreservesTokens) + updated LRUEvictionBoundsMap to reflect new security-preserving semantic. All tribal + mcpserver tests green under -race. Build clean, gofmt clean, go vet clean.
REVIEW: Performed 3-pass review (code / security / go). (1) code-reviewer: invariant 'key in at most one of {active, snapshot}' holds; traced thrash test through 2 cycles manually — victim's snapshot entry survives because it is re-pushed-front on each eviction cycle. No CRITICAL/HIGH issues. (2) security-reviewer: thrash-reset vuln (SEC-1) is defeated; residual attack requires 2*maxKeys distinct IDs AND waiting for snapshot LRU to cycle — acceptable MEDIUM mitigation with documented 2x memory cost. No new log-injection, timing, or privilege surfaces. (3) go-reviewer: idiomatic sync.Mutex+container/list LRU; *Locked helper convention; -race clean; no new imports. Verdict: clean, proceed to close. Also simplified storeSnapshotLocked — removed unreachable 'stale snapshot replace' branch (invariant prevents it).
close_reason: DONE: Fixed KeyedLimiter thrash-reset vuln via snapshot LRU cache. Evicted limiters move to bounded snapshot (size=MaxKeys) preserving *rate.Limiter pointer; re-insertion restores it. Key subtlety: pop victim's snapshot BEFORE evicting actives. 3 new tests + 1 existing test updated. All tribal/mcpserver tests green under -race. Files: extractor/tribal/limiter.go, limiter_test.go.

title: Add cosign verify-blob to install.sh curl-pipe-sh installer for parity with action.yml/prbot workflow
notes:
PLAN: Add cosign verify-blob to install.sh mirroring action.yml pattern. Download archive + checksums.txt + .sig + .pem via curl --retry. Fail-closed if any signing artifact missing. Require cosign on PATH — fail with install hint if absent (NO auto-bootstrap: self-verifying cosign download has bootstrap circularity; simpler + safer to ask user to install). Run cosign verify-blob with identity-regexp '^https://github\.com/sjarmak/livedocs/\.github/workflows/release\.yml@refs/tags/v.*$' and oidc-issuer 'https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com' — byte-identical to action.yml/prbot.yml. Then existing sha256 integrity check. No unsigned fallback. POSIX /bin/sh portable (retain current shebang). Out of scope: action.yml/release.yml (already done in 5vc.11).
PLAN-REVIEW (adversarial self-critique, Task tool unavailable in worktree — pattern from prior 5vc beads): (1) Flow matches .goreleaser.yaml: signs block artifacts: checksum emits checksums.txt.sig + checksums.txt.pem — install.sh downloads exactly those names. (2) cosign missing → fail-loud with install hint (brew/apt/go install/direct binary + recommend >=v2.0). (3) Network flake → curl --retry 3 --retry-delay 2; real 404 (older release without sigs) fails-closed via curl -f + set -e before cosign runs. (4) First-time UX: users without cosign get specific install instructions; users with it get automatic verification. (5) Footguns audited: (a) Fulcio chain + Rekor tlog checked by default in keyless mode — no flags needed, do NOT add --insecure-ignore-tlog. (b) identity-regexp + oidc-issuer copied BYTE-IDENTICAL from action.yml — drift is the killer risk. (c) REPO=sjarmak/livedocs matches identity pin. (d) grep -qF guard before sha256sum --ignore-missing -c closes --ignore-missing silent-pass bypass (mirrors 5vc.7 fix). (e) TOCTOU: TMPDIR is 0700 mktemp, archive sha256 re-verified after authenticating checksums.txt — swap would be caught. (f) No command injection — VERSION from GH API tag_name, quoted, URL path only. (6) POSIX /bin/sh: no bashisms; set -e only (no pipefail in POSIX). VERDICT: proceed.
PROGRESS: Added cosign verify-blob integration to install.sh. Flow: (1) require cosign on PATH, else fail-loud with brew/apt/go install/direct-binary hints + recommend >= v2.0; (2) download archive + checksums.txt + checksums.txt.sig + checksums.txt.pem via curl --retry 3 --retry-delay 2 (transient network resilience; real 404 fails closed under set -e); (3) fail-closed if any of the 4 files is missing or empty; (4) cosign verify-blob with identity-regexp + oidc-issuer BYTE-IDENTICAL to action.yml / examples/workflows/livedocs-prbot.yml; (5) grep -qF guard + sha256sum --ignore-missing -c (closes the sha256sum silent-pass bypass mirroring 5vc.7 fix); (6) BSD shasum fallback for macOS (no --ignore-missing) filters to the single archive entry and compares manually; (7) no fallback-to-unsigned anywhere — set -e aborts on any non-zero before extract/install. Constants lifted to top (COSIGN_IDENTITY_REGEXP, COSIGN_OIDC_ISSUER) with a comment that drift vs release pipeline silently breaks verification. Switched set -e → set -eu for stricter unset-var catch. Switched REPO canonicalization: now pins to sjarmak/livedocs (matches identity-regex). Verified: bash -n OK, sh -n OK, dash -n OK, shellcheck -s sh at style severity: zero findings. Mock-flow harness: 7/7 tests pass (missing cosign fail-loud prints all hints; cosign-failure blocks install; checksum-not-listed blocks install). Happy-path end-to-end: successful install, exit 0.

title: Rate-limit tribal_mine_on_demand per MCP session to prevent budget DoS
description: MEDIUM from Wave 1 convergence security review. tribal_mine_on_demand accepts arbitrary (repo, symbol) from any connected MCP client. DailyBudget is the only throttle — adversarial or compromised agent can exhaust daily LLM budget across all repos via rapid invocations. Fix: per-session quota + rate limit on the tool itself (token bucket); log MCP session identity alongside each budget deduction for accountability.
notes:
PLAN: Add per-MCP-session token-bucket rate limiter to tribal_mine_on_demand + bounded-keyspace LRU wrapper. Design: (1) golang.org/x/time/rate.Limiter per session keyed by server.ClientSession.SessionID() (via adapter.go to preserve mcp-go isolation). (2) LRU-bounded map of limiters (default 256 sessions; LRU evict on insert beyond cap) — prevents unbounded limiter-map DoS. (3) Anonymous/no-session clients bucket under shared 'anonymous' key with same quota — rejection would break stdio single-client mode where no session-ID exists. (4) Logging contract: every mine attempt logs {session_id, repo, symbol, outcome} so budget deductions are attributable. (5) Expose KeyedLimiter as reusable primitive in extractor/tribal package so m7v.17 can wrap its singleflight key-space to bound DoS surface. Files: extractor/tribal/limiter.go (+test), mcpserver/session_limiter.go (+test wiring in tribal_mine.go), mcpserver/adapter.go (SessionIDFromContext helper), mcpserver/server.go (Config.MineRateLimit plumbing), mcpserver/tribal_mine.go (wire limiter + session logging). Tests: single-session burst then denial, two-session isolation, anonymous-session bucket, LRU eviction (N+1 sessions -> oldest evicted), logs include session_id, DailyBudget independent of rate-limit. TDD with mock clock via rate.Limiter itself (SetLimit, time.Now not mocked — use burst exhaustion which is deterministic). Risks mitigated: (a) limiter-map DoS = LRU cap; (b) session spoofing = session-id is just a bucket key, same spoofed ID shares bucket (not privilege); (c) log-injection = quote session_id via %q; (d) restart wipes limiters = acceptable (defense-in-depth, not authz); (e) rate vs DailyBudget = orthogonal (rate caps RPS, budget caps daily cost). Out of scope: m7v.23 (factory recovery), m7v.21 (sanitizer, merged), modifying service.go beyond exposing KeyedLimiter.
PROGRESS: Implemented. extractor/tribal/limiter.go exports KeyedLimiter (bounded-LRU token-bucket). mcpserver/adapter.go adds SessionIDFromContext (confines mcp-go to adapter). mcpserver/tribal_mine.go adds TribalMineOnDemandRateLimitedHandler wrapper + MineLogger interface + attributable logging (session_id/repo/symbol/outcome). mcpserver/server.go Config gains MineRateLimiter+MineLogger (backward-compatible: nil = disabled). Tests: 7 KeyedLimiter tests (single-key burst, multi-session isolation, anonymous bucket, LRU bounds, LRU touch-on-access, refill, concurrent race) + 7 handler tests (single-session rate-limit, per-session isolation, anon bucket, logs session identity, budget-exceeded orthogonal, nil-limiter fallback, log field truncation/escape). go build, go vet, go test -race all clean. Validation perf test (scheduler.go parse >500ms under -race) is pre-existing flake unrelated to this change — passes without -race.
REVIEW (3-lens, inline — Task unavailable). code-review: API shape acceptable (4 params for wrapper; documented), naming clear, error handling matches existing tribal_mine.go contract, tests deterministic (burst-based, not clock-based). Deferred LOWs: mineOutcome nil-result case folded into transport_error (minor), safeArg wrapper kept for intent clarity. security-review: limiter-map DoS=BOUNDED via LRU maxKeys; session-spoof=bucket-key-only (no privilege); log-injection=%q escape + 256-byte truncation (fixed inline after review, covered by TestTribalMineOnDemand_LogFieldsBoundedAndEscaped); rate-vs-budget=orthogonal (verified by TestTribalMineOnDemand_BudgetExceededStillSurfaced); race-on-init=one sync.Mutex, -race clean x3 iterations. go-review: one sync.Mutex (no nested locks), O(1) warm path, no goroutine leaks, Close() a no-op-but-kept for defer idiom, go vet clean, gofmt applied. No CRITICAL/HIGH findings. One MEDIUM found during security pass (log-volume DoS via unbounded field sizes) fixed inline with maxLogFieldLen=256 + truncateForLog + new test. No new beads filed.
close_reason:
DONE. Per-session token-bucket rate limiter on tribal_mine_on_demand.

Primitive exposed:
- extractor/tribal.KeyedLimiter (NewKeyedLimiter, Allow, Limiter, Size, Close)
  with KeyedLimiterConfig {Rate, Burst, MaxKeys, AnonymousID}. Bounded-LRU
  map prevents the limiter itself from becoming a DoS surface. Stateless
  across process restarts (defense-in-depth, not authz).

Integration points:
- mcpserver.Config.MineRateLimiter and MineLogger (both optional,
  backward compatible — nil=disabled).
- mcpserver.SessionIDFromContext in adapter.go (the ONLY mcp-go session
  touchpoint; preserves CLAUDE.md adapter-isolation rule).
- mcpserver.TribalMineOnDemandRateLimitedHandler wraps the legacy
  handler; denies with a safe "rate limit exceeded" error result BEFORE
  invoking the MiningServiceFactory (zero LLM cost on denial) and
  writes attributable log lines {session_id, repo, symbol, outcome} with
  %q-escape + 256-byte truncation for log-injection + log-volume defense.

How m7v.17 should consume it:
- extractor/tribal.KeyedLimiter is in the same package as TribalMiningService
  so m7v.17 can drop it into service.go without a new import cycle.
- Recommended: wrap the singleflight key-space by calling
  limiter.Allow(relPath) at the entry to mineFileOnce (or wherever the
  singleflight.Group.Do is invoked). With MaxKeys < len(all-possible-paths)
  the LRU bound caps the attacker's ability to enumerate distinct relPaths.
- Doc-comment on KeyedLimiter already references m7v.17 explicitly so a
  future reviewer sees the intended cross-bead usage.

Test coverage:
- 7 KeyedLimiter unit tests: burst-then-deny, per-session isolation,
  anonymous-bucket sharing, LRU bound under N+1 seed, LRU touch-on-access,
  refill timing, concurrent -race.
- 7 handler integration tests: single-session rate-limit, multi-session
  isolation, anonymous bucket, logs session identity, DailyBudget still
  surfaces even under permissive limiter, nil-limiter unlimited fallback,
  hostile-session-ID log truncation + escape.
- go build, go vet, go test, go test -race all clean on
  ./extractor/tribal/ and ./mcpserver/. Full suite passes without -race;
  one unrelated pre-existing perf flake in validation/ under -race load.

Follow-ups: none filed. One MEDIUM (log-volume DoS via unbounded field
sizes) was surfaced during the security-review pass and fixed inline
with maxLogFieldLen + truncateForLog + new test. No remaining
CRITICAL/HIGH.

Commit: b165138 on branch worktree-agent-aa05f49f.
Unblocks: live_docs-m7v.17 (merge requires the limiter primitive so the
singleflight key-space can be bounded before its worktree merges).

title: Sanitize MiningResult.FailedErrors before caller-accessible surfaces
description: MEDIUM from Wave 1 convergence security review. FailedErrors []error may carry SQLite schema details (table/column names, constraint violations with raw offending values) and LLM-generated text echoing user content. No sanitization contract. Options: (a) document on field that callers MUST sanitize, or (b) change type to []string with pre-sanitized messages produced at capture time in service.go. Option (b) preferred — enforces at the boundary.
notes:
PLAN: Option (b) — change MiningResult.FailedErrors []error → []string. Add sanitizeUpsertError() producing canonical category strings (unique_constraint_violation, database_error, provenance_validation_failed, unknown_upsert_error). Log raw error server-side at capture site in MineFile so operators retain debuggability; callers never see raw SQL/paths/LLM echo. Files: extractor/tribal/service.go (type change + helper), mcpserver/tribal_mine.go (adjust one string access), tests in both. No callers use errors.Is/As on FailedErrors (verified by Grep). Coordination: no overlap with m7v.22 (rate limiting) or m7v.23 (MiningFactory probe).
PROGRESS: Added extractor/tribal/sanitize.go with sanitizeUpsertError() mapping raw errors to canonical category strings. Changed MiningResult.FailedErrors from []error to []string. MineFile now logs raw error via log.Printf at capture site (operator debuggability) and appends only the sanitized category to the result. Updated mcpserver/tribal_mine.go logMiningFailures to use string directly. Tests: 6 new test functions + updated 3 existing. go build ./..., go test ./..., go test -race all pass. go vet clean.
REVIEW: Three-lens self-review performed (code-reviewer, security-reviewer, go-reviewer). Code-reviewer: one MEDIUM found — per-failure operator logging could flood logs on pathological files. Fixed by capping operator log.Printf at maxFailedErrorsCaptured and emitting a single summary line when cap is exceeded. Security-reviewer: verified (1) sanitizer never returns raw error text, only canonical constants; (2) no path lets a raw error reach callers — MiningError uses SafeMessage() on MCP side, extract_cmd never prints Error(); (3) operator log retains full detail for debuggability; (4) log-side/caller-side separation enforced at capture site. Go-reviewer: no errors.Is/As callers on FailedErrors (verified). Idiomatic switch ladder; strings.ToLower allocation bounded by cap. Tests are table-driven. gofmt clean, go vet clean. go test ./..., go test -race ./... all pass.
close_reason: DONE: Established sanitization contract for MiningResult.FailedErrors. Changed type from []error → []string (Option b). Added extractor/tribal/sanitize.go with sanitizeUpsertError() mapping raw errors to 11 canonical category strings (nil_error, unique_constraint_violation, check_constraint_violation, foreign_key_constraint, not_null_constraint, database_busy, database_locked, database_error, provenance_validation_failed, context_canceled, upsert_failed). Boundary sits at single capture site in TribalMiningService.MineFile (extractor/tribal/service.go:337-354) — raw error is logged server-side via log.Printf for operator debuggability, only sanitized category reaches result.FailedErrors. Operator logging also capped at maxFailedErrorsCaptured with summary line on overflow. MCP adapter (mcpserver/tribal_mine.go logMiningFailures) now consumes string directly. Test coverage: extractor/tribal/sanitize_test.go has 6 new test functions covering (1) canonical category pinning, (2) leak-marker absence for SQL/path/symbol/LLM-echo/APIkey inputs, (3) end-to-end MineFile integration asserting no raw content reaches result, (4) FailedCount vs slice-cap invariant, (5) cap still applies post-conversion, (6) field type compile-check. Updated 3 existing tests to use new string type. go build ./..., go test ./..., go test -race ./extractor/tribal/ ./mcpserver/ ./cmd/livedocs/, gofmt, go vet all clean. No follow-up beads needed.

title: Wire MiningFactory into production binary (cmd/livedocs) to enable tribal_mine_on_demand
description: mcpserver/server.go now accepts Config.MiningFactory but no caller in cmd/livedocs/ constructs a Config with a non-nil factory, so tribal_mine_on_demand MCP tool is registered only in tests. Add production wiring that builds a TribalMiningService factory when LLM client + DailyBudget are configured, and pass it into mcpserver.Config. From convergence code review of live_docs-m7v.7.
notes:
PLAN: Add newMiningFactory(cfg config.Config) mcpserver.MiningServiceFactory helper in cmd/livedocs/mcp.go that (1) returns nil when Tribal.LLMEnabled is false, DailyBudget <= 0, or no LLM client is available (neither Claude CLI nor ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), (2) otherwise returns a factory that reads git remote from cdb.GetExtractionMeta().RepoRoot and constructs a tribal.NewTribalMiningService with the LLM client built via the same precedence as extract_cmd (Claude CLI first, Anthropic API second). Wire it into mcpserver.Config in the mcpCmd RunE. Test in cmd/livedocs/mcp_test.go covering: factory nil when LLMEnabled=false, factory nil when both LLM options absent, factory non-nil when Claude CLI/api key present and LLMEnabled=true.
PROGRESS: added buildMiningFactory helper in cmd/livedocs/mcp_mining_factory.go + 6 tests. Factory is nil unless (a) Tribal.LLMEnabled=true, (b) DailyBudget>0, (c) at least one LLM client path (Claude CLI or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) is reachable. Wired into mcpCmd via buildMiningFactory(tribalCfg, defaultLLMClientFactory). Repo owner/name resolved from cdb.GetExtractionMeta().RepoRoot via git remote get-url origin with a 5s timeout. go build ./... and go test ./cmd/livedocs/ ./mcpserver/ both pass.
REVIEW: self-review complete. Correctness: factory is nil-safe (3 early returns guard LLMEnabled, DailyBudget>0, llm-reachable). Security: probes (NewClaudeCLIClient, NewAnthropicClient) do NOT make network calls; API key read via os.Getenv only from within factory (never logged); budget enforcement delegated to tribal.TribalMiningService which caller already wraps. Go: fmt clean, vet clean, errors wrap with %w, context.WithTimeout scopes the git subprocess to 5s. Nil-safety: logMiningFactoryWireup correctly checks nil factory. No new flags added - config read from .livedocs.yaml matching extract_cmd pattern. All 32 tests in ./cmd/livedocs/ pass with -race, full repo suite ./... green.
close_reason: DONE: wired MiningFactory into cmd/livedocs. New cmd/livedocs/mcp_mining_factory.go defines buildMiningFactory(config.TribalConfig, llmClientFactory) mcpserver.MiningServiceFactory; returns nil unless (1) cfg.LLMEnabled=true, (2) cfg.DailyBudget>0, (3) Claude CLI on PATH OR ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set. mcp.go loads .livedocs.yaml via config.Load, passes cfg.Tribal + defaultLLMClientFactory to buildMiningFactory, and sets cfg.MiningFactory on mcpserver.Config so tribal_mine_on_demand is registered only for multi-repo + fully-configured deployments. Factory resolves RepoOwner/RepoName via cdb.GetExtractionMeta().RepoRoot + git remote get-url origin (5s timeout). 6 unit tests in mcp_mining_factory_test.go (all nil-branches + two enabled paths) + 1 testhelper file. go build + go test ./... all green including -race. title: Bump Node.js 20 GitHub Actions to v24-compatible versions (deprecation June 2026)
description: gascity workflow CI emits deprecation warnings for actions/cache@v4, actions/checkout@v4, actions/github-script@v7, actions/upload-artifact@v4 — all use Node.js 20 runtime which is forced to Node 24 by June 2, 2026. After June, these versions will fail. Track upstream action updates and bump when available. Applies to examples/workflows/livedocs-prbot.yml and consumer workflows. Identified during gascity workflow bump (live_docs-5vc.2).

title: Add per-file in-flight lock to TribalMiningService to prevent duplicate budget consumption
description: Concurrent MineFile calls for the same relPath both load cursor, both call ExtractForFile, both upsert — budget charged twice for same work. Add sync.Map per-file lock or document the limitation. From convergence security review.
notes:
PLAN: Use singleflight.Group keyed by relPath to dedup concurrent MineFile calls. Shared *MiningResult + shared error among waiters. Budget charged once. Trade-off: first callers trigger is recorded; cancellation of first caller cancels shared op. singleflight chosen over sync.Map+mutex because it auto-cleans the key on completion (no unbounded growth) and matches the share-result semantics idiomatically. Test: N=10 goroutines call MineFile on same relPath concurrently; assert ExtractForFile ran once (atomic counter on mock). Files: extractor/tribal/service.go, extractor/tribal/service_test.go, go.mod (promote x/sync to direct). Out of scope: mcpserver tribal_mine (m7v.20), FailedErrors sanitization (m7v.21).
PLAN-REVIEW: Self-performed adversarial review (Task subagent not available in this worktree). Key findings: (1) singleflight shares (val,err) cleanly; MiningError immutable so safe. (2) sync.Map+mutex rejected due to unbounded growth risk; singleflight auto-cleans. (3) Waiters receive shared result, not "already in flight" — avoids retry-loop complexity in callers. (4) Cancellation caveat: first callers ctx is authoritative; cancelling first caller cancels shared op and waiters see that error. Document this as known limitation; DoChan is future-proof escape hatch. (5) Budget charged exactly once per dedup window — fixes the bug. (6) Result returned to N callers must be treated as read-only; add doc-comment. (7) Generation counter bumps once (correct). No structural plan changes; proceed to TDD.
PROGRESS: Implemented singleflight.Group dedup on TribalMiningService.MineFile. Renamed inner body to mineFileOnce and wrapped MineFile in s.mineFileGroup.Do(relPath, ...). Added TestTribalMiningService_MineFile_ConcurrentDedup (RED confirmed at 10 gh pr list calls; GREEN at 1). go test -race passes 5x with -count. go build and go vet clean. go.mod promoted golang.org/x/sync from indirect to direct.
REVIEW: Self-performed three-perspective review (Task subagent unavailable). code-reviewer: API unchanged, error shape preserved (errors.As still works on shared *MiningError), shared result is safe (grep confirms no caller mutates returned .Facts). security-reviewer: no unbounded map growth (singleflight auto-deletes entries); relPath key is trusted internal; no info leak (same-key callers always had access); cross-caller cancellation is a documented LOW not exploitable in internal threat model. go-reviewer: go vet clean, -race clean over 3 iterations, singleflight usage idiomatic, no goroutine leak (Do runs in caller goroutine). No CRITICAL/HIGH. Shipping.
DEFERRED: Wave 2 convergence security review flagged unbounded singleflight key-space as DoS surface. Re-opened and blocked on m7v.22 (rate-limit tribal_mine_on_demand). Code is implemented and unit-tested in worktree agent-a25694db (branch worktree-agent-a25694db, commits not pushed); do not merge until m7v.22 provides the bounded-semaphore wrapper. Doc-comment MEDIUM (joiner-context cancellation phrasing) can be fixed in the same PR when this unblocks. Module path skew in the worktree (based on 3493db6 pre-rename) will auto-resolve on 3-way merge.
PLAN: Wrap MineFile in singleflight.Group (dedup concurrent same-key calls) + optional KeyedLimiter at entry (bounds key-space against synthetic-path DoS). Rename body→mineFileOnce. Add WithMineLimiter option (nil=backward compat). ErrMineThrottled sentinel + 'mine_throttled' MiningError code distinguishable from budget_exceeded. 6 tests covering dedup, budget-once, no-dedup-diff-keys, limiter-denial, nil-limiter, bounded-keyspace. Adversarial review: (a) first-caller ctx cancel propagates to waiters — documented; (b) shared MiningResult is read-only — doc-commented; (c) limiter error distinct from budget; (d) generation bumps once per dedup window.
PROGRESS: Implemented singleflight.Group + optional KeyedLimiter wiring in extractor/tribal/service.go. New: (1) ErrMineThrottled sentinel + MiningError code 'mine_throttled'. (2) TribalMiningService.mineFileGroup (singleflight) + mineLimiter (*KeyedLimiter, nil=unlimited). (3) WithMineLimiter(lim) ServiceOption. (4) MineFile wraps mineFileOnce: limiter-gate first (bounds singleflight keyspace), then singleflight.Do dedups concurrent same-key calls. 6 new tests: ConcurrentDedup (prListArrivals=1, apiCalls=1, gen=1), BudgetChargedOnce (callCount=1), DifferentKeysNoDedup (apiCalls=N for N distinct paths), LimiterDenial (mine_throttled+no extract), NilLimiterUnlimited, LimiterBoundedKeyspace (size<=maxKeys). RED verified: bypassing singleflight fails ConcurrentDedup (arrivals=10) and BudgetChargedOnce (callCount=2) + -race detects concurrent writes. GREEN: go test -race ./extractor/tribal -count=3 passes 47s. go build + go vet clean. Full ./... suite green.
REVIEW: 3-lens inline. CODE: singleflight.Do idiomatic; shared-result immutability documented; errors.As/Is/Unwrap all preserved; RED verified (ConcurrentDedup + BudgetChargedOnce fail without dedup). SECURITY: bounded-keyspace invariant proven (LimiterBoundedKeyspace seeds maxKeys+12 keys → lim.Size()<=maxKeys); mine_throttled distinct from budget_exceeded; nil-limiter=unlimited is backward-compatible; cross-session leak n/a (service has single repo binding). GO: -race clean 3x, no goroutine leaks (singleflight.Do in caller goroutine), gofmt applied, go vet clean, idiomatic error wrapping with ErrMineThrottled sentinel. No CRITICAL/HIGH. Documented MEDIUM: first-caller ctx cancel propagates to waiters (accepted trade-off, documented in doc-comment). Shipping.
close_reason:
DONE: Wrapped TribalMiningService.MineFile in singleflight.Group (keyed by relPath) + optional KeyedLimiter gate at entry. Changes confined to extractor/tribal/service.go + service_test.go.

Shipped:
- ErrMineThrottled sentinel + MiningError{Code:'mine_throttled'} distinguishable from ErrBudgetExceeded.
- TribalMiningService.mineFileGroup (singleflight) + mineLimiter (*KeyedLimiter, nil=unlimited/backward-compatible).
- WithMineLimiter(*KeyedLimiter) ServiceOption — callers wire the m7v.22 primitive.
- MineFile wrapper: (1) limiter.Allow gate (returns mine_throttled without entering singleflight or touching miner, bounding dedup keyspace); (2) singleflight.Do wraps renamed mineFileOnce (actual work).
- Doc-comment explicitly calls out: concurrent-dedup semantics, shared-result-read-only contract, first-caller-ctx cancellation caveat, optional rate-limit gate.

Tests (6 new):
- ConcurrentDedup — N=10 same key → prListArrivals=1, apiCalls=1, gen=1.
- BudgetChargedOnce — N=10 same key → miner.callCount=1.
- DifferentKeysNoDedup — 10 distinct keys → apiCalls=10.
- LimiterDenial — Burst=1, second call → mine_throttled, no extract.
- NilLimiterUnlimited — 20 rapid distinct calls all succeed.
- LimiterBoundedKeyspace — maxKeys=8 + 20 minted → lim.Size()<=8.

Verification: RED confirmed by temporarily bypassing singleflight (ConcurrentDedup fails with arrivals=10, BudgetChargedOnce fails with callCount=2 + race detector fires). GREEN at go test -race ./extractor/tribal -count=3 (43s). Full ./... suite clean, gofmt/vet clean.

Bounded-keyspace DoS surface (the reason m7v.17 was deferred behind m7v.22): now bounded by the caller-supplied KeyedLimiter's MaxKeys LRU. Callers that trust relPath (in-process CLI) pass nil; callers exposed to untrusted input (MCP) should install a limiter — wiring deferred to follow-up (not in this bead's scope). Backward compat: existing NewTribalMiningService callers unchanged.

Follow-up for main session: wire WithMineLimiter into mcpserver's service-construction site (outside this bead's scope — follow-on bead or handled by m7v.23 parallel work).

Tests: all-success, all-fail (inject duplicate subject_id/kind via pre-seeded fact? simpler: use ClaimsDB that closes early, forcing upsert errors), mixed. Cap prevents unbounded growth on pathological files.
PROGRESS: MiningResult now surfaces FailedCount and FailedErrors (capped at 32). Added factUpserter interface + claimsDBUpserter adapter as a narrow test seam. MineFile iterates ALL facts (no early abort) and records per-fact upsert errors. Generation counter only bumps when at least one fact written. 3 new tests cover: all-fail, mixed, cap enforcement. Full tribal test suite passes with -race.
REVIEW: 3-perspective review complete (code-reviewer, security-reviewer, go-reviewer). Findings: no CRITICAL/HIGH. (1) correctness: loop iterates all facts, generation counter only bumps on success, additive fields default to zero values so cmd/livedocs/extract_cmd.go caller unchanged; (2) security: no new leak surface — errors wrap pre-existing DB errors that were silently dropped before; 32-item cap bounds memory; (3) go idioms: []error is idiomatic (callers may want to iterate); factUpserter is a 1-method interface defined where used; withFactUpserter is unexported test-only option; naming follows noun-er convention. gofmt + go vet clean; all tests pass with -race.
REVIEW: 3-perspective review complete (code-reviewer, security-reviewer, go-reviewer). No CRITICAL/HIGH findings. close_reason: DONE: MiningResult now exposes FailedCount (authoritative total) + FailedErrors ([]error, capped at 32) so MineFile no longer silently drops UpsertTribalFact failures. title: tribal_mine_on_demand MCP tool (JIT mining)
description:
New MCP tool at mcpserver/tribal_mine.go: tribal_mine_on_demand(symbol, repo) runs PR comment mining for files containing the given symbol using PRCommentMiner + UpsertTribalFact. Returns newly-mined facts with full provenance envelopes. Co-equal with batch path per convergence debate.

## Context
PRD S2. Depends on M7 (TribalMiningService) for shared orchestration. Uses M3 incremental cursor (source_files.last_pr_id_set) for idempotency.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Calling tool with symbol + repo triggers PR miner for files containing that symbol
2. Facts inserted via UpsertTribalFact with full provenance
3. Respects DailyBudget
4. Second call on same symbol returns cached results, zero LLM calls (shared M3 cursor)
5. Integration test with fake CommandRunner + mock LLM asserts fact count + call count on second invocation
notes:
PLAN: Goal: implement tribal_mine_on_demand MCP tool (S2) that agents invoke with (symbol, repo) to trigger JIT PR-comment mining for files containing that symbol, delegating to existing TribalMiningService.MineSymbol. Approach: (1) New file mcpserver/tribal_mine.go with TribalMineOnDemandHandler and TribalMineOnDemandToolDef; (2) Introduce a narrow MiningServiceFactory func type in mcpserver so handlers can construct a service per-request using the right DB+LLM+runner without importing LLM infra; the factory is injected into the tool def; (3) Register tool in server.go registerMultiRepoTools when factory is non-nil (keeps factory optional); (4) Handler resolves repo -> ClaimsDB via pool.Open, calls factory to build TribalMiningService, invokes MineSymbol(ctx, symbol, TriggerJITOnDemand), maps results to []tribalFactEnvelope using existing factToEnvelope + validateProvenanceEnvelope, returns JSON {symbol, repo, facts, total}. Files to touch: mcpserver/tribal_mine.go (new), mcpserver/tribal_mine_test.go (new), mcpserver/server.go (wire factory + register tool), mcpserver/adapter.go (NO new mcp-go; just the handler which uses existing interfaces). mcp-go imports remain confined to adapter.go (verified). Tests: integration test uses tribal.NewTribalMiningService via the factory with a fake CommandRunner and mock LLM (local mcpserver-scoped fakes). Asserts: (1) first call returns >=1 fact, LLM calls > 0; (2) second call on same symbol returns 0 new facts AND mockLLM.callCount unchanged (cursor idempotency); (3) budget-exhausted call returns structured error-text result without panic; (4) facts include full provenance envelope; (5) repo validation rejects path traversal. Risks: R1 race on concurrent same-symbol calls (acknowledged, deferred to m7v.17); R2 LLMClient interface duplication — mitigated by depending directly on tribal.LLMClient; R3 factory indirection could grow — keep signature minimal (ctx, repo, *db.ClaimsDB) -> (*tribal.TribalMiningService, error). Out of scope: per-file lock (m7v.17), new service methods (file discovery bead if needed), LLM client wiring in prod Serve path (the factory is only wired when explicitly enabled).
PLAN-REVIEW: Self-administered architect adversarial review (Task agent unavailable). Findings folded: (1) Handle nil/empty MineSymbol results explicitly — emit 'no source files found for symbol' message; (2) User-facing errors use MiningError.SafeMessage() not Error() to avoid leaking internal paths; (3) Factory signature minimized to (repo string, cdb *db.ClaimsDB) -> (*tribal.TribalMiningService, error); (4) Register tool only when factory != nil — non-breaking for existing server.New callers; (5) Idempotency test shares DB file + mock LLM across calls (cursor lives in DB, not service); (6) Input validation: pool.Open already calls validateRepoName — no additional injection vector since symbol is parameterized SQL and file paths come from our trusted symbol table; (7) Race condition for concurrent same-symbol calls already tracked by live_docs-m7v.17 — no new bead needed; (8) ZFC compliance: handler is pure plumbing; (9) mcp-go imports confirmed confined to adapter.go; (10) MiningError.Code-based branching in handler for budget_exceeded / symbol_resolution_failed / extraction_failed.
TEST-REVIEW: Self-administered code-reviewer of staged mcpserver/tribal_mine_test.go (code-reviewer agent unavailable). Findings: (1) All 5 ACs covered — FirstCallMinesFacts tests AC1+AC2; IdempotentSecondCall tests AC4+AC5; BudgetExceeded tests AC3 (strengthened to assert LLM calls <= 2 with DailyBudget=1 AND short safe error messages ≤ 512 chars); (2) Provenance envelope validation asserts Body, SourceQuote, Kind, Status=active, Extractor, LastVerified, Evidence all populated; (3) Added defensive tests: MissingRepo, UnknownSymbol, MissingParams (table-driven), PathTraversalRepo, FactoryError — these cover the security surface the security-reviewer will examine; (4) Race-safe LLM mock via atomic.Int64 for Calls(); (5) The idempotency test shares DB file via pool + shared LLM mock across calls — second-call cursor read comes from DB, not service state; (6) Tests depend on still-unimplemented symbols MiningServiceFactory, TribalMineOnDemandHandler, tribalMineResponse — build failure confirms RED state.
PROGRESS: Implemented mcpserver/tribal_mine.go with TribalMineOnDemandHandler + TribalMineOnDemandToolDef. Introduced MiningServiceFactory func type so mcpserver stays decoupled from LLM-client wiring; factory is called once per handler invocation to build a tribal.TribalMiningService bound to the repo's ClaimsDB. title: Sampling-based semantic reverification CLI
description:
New CLI: livedocs tribal reverify --sample=N --max-age=30d. Samples active LLM-extracted facts older than max-age, runs one Haiku call per fact asking 'does this fact still describe this code?', applies verdict. Budget-tracked against DailyBudget. ~40-line addition to drift/tribal.go.

## Context
PRD S1. Bounded (explicit --sample=N), off hot path (manual/cron), operates on aged facts only. Addresses Q1 from Phase 1 PRD (semantic drift detection without LLM-per-commit).
acceptance_criteria:
1. Samples at most N active LLM-extracted facts (model != '') older than max-age
2. Accept verdict: touch last_verified
3. Downgrade verdict: confidence *= 0.6
4. Reject verdict: status = 'stale'
5. Budget-tracked against DailyBudget
6. go test ./drift/... -run TribalReverify passes with all three verdict paths
notes:
PLAN: Add ReverifyTribal() to drift/tribal.go with SemanticVerifier interface (injectable LLM). Samples N active facts with model!="" older than max-age. Three verdict paths: accept→touch last_verified, downgrade→confidence*=0.6, reject→status=stale. Budget tracked via call counter (fail-stop). Need 2 new DB methods: UpdateFactLastVerified, UpdateFactConfidence. CLI: tribal reverify --sample=N --max-age=30d in cmd/livedocs/tribal_cmd.go. Tests in drift/tribal_reverify_test.go covering all 3 verdicts + budget exhaustion + sampling bounds.
PROGRESS: Implemented ReverifyTribal in drift/tribal_reverify.go with SemanticVerifier interface, 3 verdict paths, budget tracking. Added UpdateFactLastVerified, UpdateFactConfidence, GetActiveLLMFactsOlderThan to db/tribal.go. CLI subcommand tribal reverify with --sample, --max-age, --budget, --dry-run flags. All 8 tests pass covering accept/downgrade/reject/budget/sampling/deterministic-skip/recent-skip/mixed.
REVIEW: Three-perspective review completed. No CRITICAL or HIGH issues. MEDIUM: context.Background() in ReverifyTribal matches existing CheckTribal pattern. Code is consistent with existing drift/ and db/ patterns. Tests cover all 8 acceptance criteria scenarios. Security: no secrets, no prompt injection surface in this scope, input validation on all CLI flags.
close_reason: DONE: Implemented sampling-based semantic reverification. New files: drift/tribal_reverify.go (ReverifyTribal function with SemanticVerifier interface, 3 verdict paths: accept->touch last_verified, downgrade->confidence*=0.6, reject->stale), drift/tribal_reverify_test.go (8 tests covering all verdicts, budget exhaustion, sample bounds, deterministic-skip, recent-skip). Modified: db/tribal.go (3 new methods: UpdateFactLastVerified, UpdateFactConfidence, GetActiveLLMFactsOlderThan), cmd/livedocs/tribal_cmd.go (tribal reverify subcommand with --sample, --max-age, --budget, --dry-run flags; LLM verifier wiring deferred to follow-up bead). Key decisions: (1) SemanticVerifier is an interface for test injection and future LLM wiring, (2) CLI returns error for non-dry-run until LLM verifier is wired, (3) Budget is a simple call counter matching existing patterns.

title: Extract shared resetCmdFlags(cmd *cobra.Command) helper; replace per-command duplicates
description: Follow-up from wave 2 code review (m7v.33). resetEnrichFlags (cmd/livedocs/enrich.go) and resetVerifyClaimsFlags (cmd/livedocs/verify_claims.go) have identical bodies today — pflag.VisitAll → Value.Set(DefValue) + Changed=false, with stderr logging on Set error. As more commands get the defer-reset pattern (to unblock m7v.35's root-cause refactor or before it), this will become N copies of the same function. Extract a single resetCmdFlags(cmd *cobra.Command) helper (likely in a new cmd/livedocs/flags.go or similar) and call it from each RunE's deferred reset. Low priority — pure cleanup, no behavior change. Blocks eventual application to check/diff/export/tribal_cmd commands.
notes:
