PLAN: Bodies of resetEnrichFlags and resetVerifyClaimsFlags are byte-identical (pflag.VisitAll → Value.Set(DefValue) + Changed=false, with stderr warning on Set error). Approach: (1) Create cmd/livedocs/flags.go with resetCmdFlags(cmd *cobra.Command) helper that takes a *cobra.Command and writes warnings to cmd.ErrOrStderr() (better than os.Stderr — testable, also covers cobra's stderr override). (2) Replace defer resetEnrichFlags(cmd) and defer resetVerifyClaimsFlags(cmd) with defer resetCmdFlags(cmd). (3) Delete the now-dead wrapper functions to avoid indirection (KISS — no behavior preserved that callers depend on; both are unexported and have no other callers). Files: NEW cmd/livedocs/flags.go + flags_test.go; EDIT enrich.go (delete resetEnrichFlags, switch defer); EDIT verify_claims.go (delete resetVerifyClaimsFlags, switch defer). Test plan: build a synthetic *cobra.Command with string/bool/int flags, mutate them, set Changed, capture cmd.ErrOrStderr() via SetErr(buf), call helper, assert defaults restored, Changed cleared. Set-error path: register a flag with a Value whose Set always errors (custom pflag.Value implementation), assert warning written to err buffer. Risks: (a) switching from os.Stderr to cmd.ErrOrStderr() is a minor observable change but no test asserts on the destination; cmd.ErrOrStderr() defaults to os.Stderr so behavior matches in production. (b) Leaving wrappers as shims would add no value — both are package-internal and not referenced in tests. Out of scope per bead: check/diff/export/tribal_cmd defer additions (m7v.35), changes to package-level flag vars (m7v.35), mcpserver.

Wrappers add zero value. (3) Error semantics: original swallows the error after logging (returns nothing); helper does same. (4) Changed=false applied even on Set error — preserves original behavior. (5) No comment-out or history left behind — wrappers fully deleted. (6) 'see m7v.28' comment on enrich.go's defer references the removed resetVerifyClaimsFlags; will update to point at the new helper. No changes to plan.

title: Test first-caller ctx-cancellation propagation through MineFile singleflight
description: Wave 3 convergence code review (MEDIUM CR1) on live_docs-m7v.17. TribalMiningService.MineFile wraps mineFileOnce in singleflight.Do, and the doc-comment correctly notes that the first caller's context is authoritative — its cancellation aborts the shared op and all waiters see that error. This trade-off is documented but NOT tested. A regression that silently swapped singleflight for per-call execution would pass all existing tests. Add TestTribalMiningService_MineFile_FirstCallerCancelPropagates: N=3 goroutines call MineFile on same relPath concurrently; when the first caller's ctx is cancelled mid-extract (at runner.barrier), assert all N see context.Canceled (or wrapped form) and the miner's callCount is 1.
notes:
PLAN: Reuse countingRunner/barrier; add ctxAware variant so runner returns ctx.Err() when first caller's ctx is cancelled (mimicking real IO cancel). N=3: g0 has cancellable ctx, g1/g2 use Background (per singleflight semantics, only g0's ctx is authoritative). waitForSettle to ensure all 3 goroutines entered MineFile and 1 at barrier. Cancel g0 ctx. Assert all 3 see context.Canceled via errors.Is (wrapped in MiningError), prListArrivals==1 (dedup), apiCalls==0 (extract aborted pre-LLM). Files: extractor/tribal/service_test.go only.
PLAN-REVIEW: Self-adversarial audit passed with fixes: (1) Must use wgStarted WaitGroup AND verify prListArrivals==1 before cancelling, to ensure g1/g2 are actually parked in singleflight.Do rather than still racing to enter. (2) Runner must be ctx-aware so cancel wakes the barrier block, else test hangs. (3) errors.Is traversal: *MiningError -> fmt wrap -> context.Canceled (3 levels). (4) Regression signals: removing singleflight yields prListArrivals==3 (fail); swapping for per-call yields g1/g2 succeed (fail 'all 3 see Canceled'). Both regressions are detected.
PROGRESS: Added TestTribalMiningService_MineFile_FirstCallerCancelPropagates. Passes under -race with -count 10 stable. Key design: (a) Added ctxAware field to countingRunner so barrier wait honors ctx.Done (required so first caller's cancel propagates through runner IO). (b) Serialize g0 first: wait for g0 to arrive at barrier BEFORE launching waiters, because singleflight 'first caller' is whichever goroutine wins the race into Do() (no ordering guarantee). Launching all N concurrently made the test flaky under load. (c) Assertions: all N see errors.Is(err, context.Canceled), results all nil, prListArrivals==1 (dedup), miner.callCount==0 (budget not charged), FactsGeneration==0 (no writes). Regression signals: removing singleflight breaks arrivals==1 AND 'all see Canceled' simultaneously.
REVIEW: Three-pass review complete. code-reviewer: acceptance criteria met (N=3, cancel mid-extract, all see context.Canceled, prListArrivals==1 proves dedup). Noted bead's literal callCount==1 vs our callCount==0 discrepancy: our cancel hits at pr list step, before classifyComment increments callCount; dedup is proven by prListArrivals==1 instead. security-reviewer: no goroutine leaks (wgDone.Wait + t.Cleanup closeBarrierOnce via atomic.Bool), no fd leaks, no double-close risk. go-reviewer: idiomatic WaitGroup/select/ctx patterns, race-clean -count 10. Regression sim verified: removed singleflight causes test to time out with waiters parked at barrier -- correct failure mode. No new beads filed.
close_reason: DONE: Added TestTribalMiningService_MineFile_FirstCallerCancelPropagates to extractor/tribal/service_test.go. The test serializes N=3 MineFile callers so g0 (with cancellable ctx) becomes the singleflight leader before g1/g2 (Background ctx) join as waiters, then cancels g0 mid-extract at the barrier. Asserts (a) all 3 errors satisfy errors.Is(err, context.Canceled), (b) all 3 results are nil, (c) prListArrivals==1 (direct singleflight dedup evidence), (d) miner.callCount==0 (budget not charged), (e) FactsGeneration==0. Regression signal verified: replacing singleflight.Do with direct mineFileOnce makes the test timeout (waiters parked at barrier with non-cancellable ctx). Also extended countingRunner with ctxAware field so barrier wait honors ctx.Done (default false preserves existing tests). -race -count 10 clean.

title: MiningFactory: recover from transient LLM-probe failure at MCP startup
description: LOW from Wave 1 convergence security review. buildMiningFactory probes LLM client availability at wire-up. Transient probe failure (temporary PATH issue, transient network error, malformed env) returns nil factory → tool never registered for process lifetime. Closure re-resolves per call but is never reached. Fix: register the tool unconditionally when LLMEnabled+budget configured; move availability check into the factory invocation; return an error response if client unreachable at call time. Also fix logMiningFactoryWireup — conflates 'not configured' with 'probe failed'.
notes:
PLAN: Remove wire-up LLM probe in buildMiningFactory; return non-nil factory whenever LLMEnabled && DailyBudget>0. Move probe into closure (already re-resolves per call; just drop the guard). Split logMiningFactoryWireup into 3 distinct branches (disabled:llm_enabled=false / disabled:daily_budget<=0 / enabled:per-call probe). Enrich MCP handler to surface LLM-unreachable error distinctly (not generic 'mining service unavailable'). Adversarial review: probe calls are local-only (LookPath + struct ctor), <1ms per call, no caching needed; no API-key leakage (never logged); no race (factory closure is stateless). Out of scope: caching, metrics, mcpserver redesign.
PROGRESS: Implemented. (1) buildMiningFactory no longer probes LLM at wire-up; returns non-nil factory whenever LLMEnabled && DailyBudget>0. (2) Factory closure wraps mcpserver.ErrLLMClientUnavailable when neither Claude CLI nor ANTHROPIC_API_KEY resolve at call time. (3) TribalMineOnDemandHandler classifies ErrLLMClientUnavailable into actionable 'LLM client unreachable — ensure claude CLI is on PATH or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set' message; non-LLM factory errors keep the generic 'mining service unavailable' message. (4) logMiningFactoryWireup split into 3 distinct lines (llm_enabled=false / daily_budget<=0 / enabled+probe-per-call); testable via logMiningFactoryWireupTo(logger, ...). (5) Added ErrLLMClientUnavailable sentinel in mcpserver/tribal_mine.go (so it lives with MiningServiceFactory); cmd/livedocs factory wraps it. Tests: 9 cmd/livedocs mining factory tests (6 existing updated + 3 new + 1 split for repo-owner-resolve) + 2 mcpserver handler tests (FactoryError + LLMUnavailableError). go build, go vet, go test clean. go test -race ./cmd/livedocs/ ./mcpserver/ -count=1 green. Full ./... green. Pre-existing flake: TestVerifyClaims* fail under -count=2 due to rootCmd global state leakage — filed live_docs-m7v.27 (unrelated to this bead; reproduces on 378f279 baseline). go test -race -count=2 also hits that flake.
REVIEW: 3-lens inline. CODE: correctness OK (non-nil factory when config ok; %w wraps sentinel so errors.Is works; %s preserves message); API clean (ErrLLMClientUnavailable in mcpserver co-located with MiningServiceFactory, no circular deps); error wrapping — non-LLM factory errors logged server-side with full cause, clients get generic message; LLM-unavailable gets actionable message naming both recovery paths. Test coverage: 9 factory tests + 2 handler tests covering registered-despite-probe-fail, call-time-unreachable, call-time-success, API-fallback, repo-owner-error (non-LLM) does not get misclassified, 3-way log split, generic-factory-error does NOT surface LLM guidance, LLM-unavailable DOES surface it. SECURITY: API key never logged (literal strings only); caller-facing message reveals no paths/env/PIDs; no TOCTOU introduced (any post-probe LLM failure surfaces via existing MiningError path); no log injection (server-only log messages use only factory-internal data). GO: context.WithTimeout+cancel correct; no goroutines; errors.Is-friendly sentinel; 1-method llmClient interface; fmt/vet clean. NO CRITICAL/HIGH. One MEDIUM kept as intentional defensive fallback: the default case in logMiningFactoryWireupTo logs 'unexpected nil factory' rather than silently no-oping — documented as defensive (buildMiningFactory cannot reach it given current config-branch structure). Inline simplify pass removed dead 'var factory interface{}' and collapsed the paired-if to single logMiningFactoryWireupTo call. Pre-existing m7v.27 flake (TestVerifyClaims under -count=2) left to dedicated bead. All tests green: go build ./..., go vet ./..., go test ./..., go test -race ./cmd/livedocs/ ./mcpserver/ -count=1.
close_reason: DONE: (1) buildMiningFactory returns non-nil whenever LLMEnabled && DailyBudget>0 — no more wire-up-time probe. (2) Factory closure wraps mcpserver.ErrLLMClientUnavailable sentinel on call-time unreachability so handler can errors.Is-classify it.
